steps.title: Keep preposition attached
steps.content: The sentence says the animals had been confiscated from the private collections of aristocrats. The word from is a preposition, and the private collections is its object, so no punctuation should separate them.

steps.title: Remove needless punctuation
steps.content: A inserts a semicolon between a preposition and its object. C uses a colon even though the words before it do not form a complete independent clause. D adds a comma that interrupts the prepositional phrase. The sentence needs the plain preposition with no punctuation. The answer is B.
